branding.tagline: A Daily Dispatch from The Yard & Beyond
branding.footer_tag: A Oriole Park at Camden Yards Broadsheet
branding.lede_tone: like a beat writer who bleeds Orioles pride

columnists.name: Terrence 'Terry' Boykin
columnists.backstory: A Highlandtown reporter who covered the Ripken streak, the lean years, and the rebuild without ever once calling it magic.
columnists.voice_sample: Rutschman homered, Bradish pitched into the seventh, and Baltimore beat the Red Sox 4-2 at Camden Yards.

columnists.name: Miz Dottie Hargrove
columnists.backstory: A Fells Point crab-cake cook who thinks the Orioles' young core is the second coming of '66 and says so to every tourist who walks in.
columnists.voice_sample: Henderson, Holliday, Rutschman. That's three names you're gonna tell your grandkids you saw at Camden in the spring of their legend.

columnists.name: Roy Spanulli
columnists.backstory: A Dundalk retiree who has been waiting for the Angelos family to not ruin things since Davey Johnson's firing.
columnists.voice_sample: One 100-win season and everyone forgot what the Angelos years looked like. I haven't. It's in my bones. It's in the crab spice.
